violations.text: Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/07/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The customer bathroom floors and walls have an accumulation of soil residue and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager certification course. The person in charge provided proof on enrollment in a food safety manger certification course. Please email inspector proof upon completion of course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

A cheeseburger was observed held at 130 degrees Fahrenheit in the sandwich hot hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, cheeseburger was cooked about an hour prior to inspection. Person in charge elected to dispose the cheeseburger (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed at 132 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. The ambient air temperature was adjusted and observed at 151 degrees Fahrenheit after adjustment at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/10/2024

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety managers certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety managers course. New Violation. Correct By: 12/11/2024

An employee was observed eating while preparing food. EMPLOYEE shall eat, drink, or use any form of tobacco only in designated areas where the contamination of exposed FOOD; clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, and LINENS; unwrapped SINGLE-SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES; or other items needing protection can not result. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

Chicken sandwiches and cheeseburgers were observed held at 121-128 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was reheated to 140-155 during time of inspection . The ambient air temperature was observed at 115 during time of visit. The temperature setting was increased during time of visit and reached 153. Establishment must set the temperature to at least 135 degrees or above. In addition, hot hold unit must be preheated to at least 135 before food is placed in the hot hold unit. Temperatures must be monitored. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

Food was observed held at 43-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the open air cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food had been out of temperature for an unknown amount of time. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed with a fluctuating temperature of 40-50 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Open air cooler was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Open air cooler must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

The ambient air temperature of the hot hold unit used to store mini tacos and boneless wings was observed at 130 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Establishment must preheat the hot hold unit to 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above when hot holding food. Establishment must monitor the temperature of the hot hold unit. If food can not be held at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above, establishment must not use hot hold unit. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by flies near the soda and slurpee machine. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment must schedule pest control services. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

An insect light trap was observed stored in a single-service article storage area and food storage area. Insect control devices must not be installed near single-service article storage areas and food storage/prep areas. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
Soda syrup was observed stored on the floor in the near the 3-bay sink. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the soda dispenser drip tray and chute have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2024
The dumpster enclosure was observed with an accumulation of debris. Dumpster enclosure must be cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Burritos and sandwiches were observed held between 46-51 degrees Fahrenheit in the open air cooler. Items were stocked pass the fill line on the shelving 3 hours prior to inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food moved to walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023

Single/self-service baked goods in the food establishment are lacking ingredient lists. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of open display cooler's shelving and the cheese/chili dispenser was observed with an accumulation of food debris. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
